Happy Monday!=C2=A0Damn it feels good to be an American.=20
Speaking of feeling good, today=E2=80=99s essay is on one of the least bori=
ng trends I=E2=80=99ve ever written about. Mental illness is an epidemic, a=
nd we have been fighting it with antiquated and suboptimal tools. The solut=

disposal for decades.=20

cs=E2=80=99 efficacy in treating mental health disorders and the companies =

 in Q4 and guaranteeing a minimum of 50 pickups for each story. For those o=

Now let=E2=80=99s get to it=E2=80=A6=20
The Magic of Mushrooms
Let=E2=80=99s Take a Trip
What do you think of when you think of psychedelics?
Maybe you think of hippies in the 1960s. Maybe you think of Phish concerts.=
 Maybe you think about that one time in college. Maybe you just think: thos=
e are drugs, they=E2=80=99re illegal, they=E2=80=99re dangerous, and they=
=E2=80=99re bad. Just Say No.
To steal a phrase from Michael Pollan=E2=80=99s bestselling 2018 book on th=

Harvard shut down the project in 1962, but Leary continued his work spreadi=
ng the word about mushrooms and LSD. In 1966, he famously told America=E2=
=80=99s youth to =E2=80=9CTurn on, tune in, drop out.=E2=80=9D Despite plea=
ding from serious researchers who feared that Leary was going to =E2=80=9Cw=
reak havoc on all of us doing LSD work all over the nation,=E2=80=9D Leary =
pushed on, like the friend who gets too high and gets everyone in trouble. =
According to Pollan, Leary said things like =E2=80=9CLSD is more frightenin=
g than the bomb,=E2=80=9D and =E2=80=9CThe kids who take LSD aren=E2=80=99t=
 going to fight your wars. They=E2=80=99re not going to join your corporati=
ons.=E2=80=9D=C2=A0
The FDA didn=E2=80=99t like the sound of that, ordering in 1966 that resear=
chers halt all work with psychedelics. Neither did Tricky Dick. In 1970, in=
 large part to slow down the anti-war counterculture movement, the Nixon go=
vernment passed the Controlled Substances Act, which classified psilocybin =
and LSD as Schedule One drugs, which have no medical use and a high potenti=
al for abuse. In 1971 Richard Nixon declared the War on Drugs and labeled L=
eary =E2=80=9Cthe most dangerous man in America.=E2=80=9D
When Harper=E2=80=99s writer Dan Baum tracked Nixon=E2=80=99s domestic poli=
cy chief John Ehrlichman down in 1994, Erlichman admitted [https://email.mg=
2.substack.com/c/eJxFUMuOwyAM_Jpy24hHaMmBw172NyIeJkElEBnSKvv1S9vDSpYfY1ujGW=
caLAVPvZfayFEB5-g1u3Il5DgRr0fPlFQk1jkgwGZi0g0PIPthU3SmxZLfH5RySVY9jXCzwYVAg=
5mEdMoEp64TFUzwYKUlL57ZHD5CdqDhAXiWDCTptbW9XsT3hf_0WA3ugHUouPTJoFvjA3rHKbv2=
QseeEiwmxV_4iu3LpNQREjWn_YTRiQmpBB_YIDMuTzTMeriMdFv4UA9bm3H3wZWNoM6l2YIxL31=
9X80G6b3oAudetyPHds6QjU3gP9rbx623lHbuoDM8a4LWAD_gyxBFJ85vN9LZfOm-5X-iP1rafp=
s] that the War on Drugs was an indirect way to target the two enemies of t=
he Nixon administration: the antiwar movement and Black people. =E2=80=9CDi=
d we know we were lying about drugs? Of course we did.=E2=80=9D=C2=A0
In a total narc move, the US also pushed other countries to follow its drug=
 scheduling guidelines. The 1971 UN treaty, the Convention on Psychotropic =
Substances, largely adopted the US scheduling, grouping psilocybin with dru=
gs like heroin and crack cocaine and effectively halting research on its be=
nefits worldwide.=C2=A0
Despite being built atop a lie, the War on Drugs and its associated propaga=
nda worked for decades. It=E2=80=99s why we=E2=80=99re just getting back to=
 psychedelics now.=C2=A0
When I was in 7th grade, we had to read a book. I forget what it was called=
 or what it was about; the only thing I remember is that the main character=
=E2=80=99s older brother did acid once, lived in a flop house, and had freq=
uent flashbacks in which spiders crawled all over his body for years.
It surprised me to find out many years and =E2=80=9Cno acid for me, thanks!=
=E2=80=9Ds later that LSD and mushrooms are the two safest drugs out there =
on the active/lethal dose and dependence potential scale. All weekend, I wa=
s drinking beers and coffee; turns out I would have been much safer trippin=
g.=C2=A0
The War on Drugs was like the Food Pyramid on crack, tricking generations o=
f Americans into making unhealthy decisions despite the best intentions. Ni=
xon started it, and Raegan picked up where he left off, waging an all-out m=
edia offensive. In 1987, the Partnership for a Drug-Free America dropped th=
e classic =E2=80=9CThis is Your Brain on Drugs=E2=80=9D commercial.=C2=A0
Lied to again! A 2014 Proceedings of the Royal Interface [https://email.mg2=
=2Esubstack.com/c/eJxFkEGOwyAMRU9TlgicpK=
ELFrOZa0QEnAQNgQqcqXL7cdPFSMjI_rae_b0=
jXEs97bM0EkfDOsVg9R1MN_QPEWwftBmMiG1aKuLuYrJUDxTPY07RO4olXxNKwSA26-AeHCD4eR=
zQ6WUZl9GguqPpVADgOeZM7ggRs0eLv1jPklEkuxE92637usE3v1pOl1rxEem8UG2LeZWlriyGE=
jlqJbV6mHdzi4sEpXupzNiJaEFxxqLuBtOB1HLIdX1Vp-eAt17tK8h2zI2c_5G-7KLaXGgulREs=
_2xux3QJjJ74348c6Zwwuzlh-BhAH8uue3hHtBlfLSER1k_x7YpRD4BxFEwLhc3L_6A_99aANQ]=
 study [https://email.mg2.substack.com/c/eJxFkEGOwyAMRU9TlgicpKELFrOZa0QEnA=
QNgQqcqXL7cdPFSMjI_rae_b0jXEs97bM0EkfDOsVg9R1MN_QPEWwftBmMiG1aKuLuYrJUDxTPY=
07RO4olXxNKwSA26-AeHCD4eRzQ6WUZl9GguqPpVADgOeZM7ggRs0eLv1jPklEkuxE92637usE3=

tK8h2zI2c_5G-7KLaXGgulREs_2xux3QJjJ74348c6Zwwuzlh-BhAH8uue3hHtBlfLSER1k_x7Y=
pRD4BxFEwLhc3L_6A_99aANQ] actually looked at the brain on drugs, psilocybin=
 specifically, and discovered that this is your brain on drugs:
Far from killing brain cells, psilocybin rewires the brain and forms rich n=
ew connections, at least temporarily, between parts of the brains that don=
=E2=80=99t normally speak to each other. That=E2=80=99s why people report s=
eeing music or tasting colors when they=E2=80=99re tripping.=C2=A0
The bad press and propaganda surrounding mushrooms, largely a remnant of th=
e culture wars of the early 1970s and late 1980s, obfuscates their incredib=
le therapeutic promise.=C2=A0

d we know we were lying about drugs? Of course we did.” </p><p s=
tyle=3D"color: #1a1a1a; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 1em=
 0;">In a total narc move, the US also pushed other countries to follow its=

opic Substances, largely adopted the US scheduling, grouping psilocybin wit=
h drugs like heroin and crack cocaine and effectively halting research on i=
ts benefits worldwide. </p><p style=3D"color: #1a1a1a; font-size: 16px=
; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 1em 0;">Despite being built atop a lie, th=
e War on Drugs and its associated propaganda worked for decades. It’s=
 why we’re just getting back to psychedelics now. </p><p style=
=3D"color: #1a1a1a; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 1em 0;"=
>When I was in 7th grade, we had to read a book. I forget what it was calle=
d or what it was about; the only thing I remember is that the main characte=
r’s older brother did acid once, lived in a flop house, and had frequ=
ent flashbacks in which spiders crawled all over his body for years.</p><p =
style=3D"color: #1a1a1a; font-size: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 1e=
m 0;">It surprised me to find out many years and “no acid for me, tha=
nks!”s later that LSD and mushrooms are the two safest drugs out ther=
e on the active/lethal dose and dependence potential scale. All weekend, I =
was drinking beers and coffee; turns out I would have been much safer tripp=
ing. </p><div class=3D"captioned-image-container-static" style=3D"font=
